examples.title: Gauss-Jordan Method
examples.description: A pseudocode implementation of the Gauss-Jordan method for solving systems of linear equations.
examples.code:
FUNCTION GaussJordan(A, b)
  // Transform A into reduced row echelon form
  FOR i FROM 1 TO n
    // Search for pivot element
    FOR j FROM i TO n
      IF A[j, i] != 0 THEN
        // Swap rows i and j
        SWAP(A[i], A[j])
        // Eliminate elements below pivot
        FOR k FROM i + 1 TO n
          A[k, i] = A[k, i] / A[i, i]
          FOR l FROM i + 1 TO n
            A[k, l] = A[k, l] - A[k, i] * A[i, l]
        END FOR
      END IF
    END FOR
  END FOR
  // Solve for variables
  FOR i FROM n TO 1
    x[i] = A[i, n + 1] / A[i, i]
    FOR j FROM i - 1 TO 1
      A[j, n + 1] = A[j, n + 1] - A[j, i] * x[i]
    END FOR
  END FOR
  RETURN x
END FUNCTION
examples.explanation: This pseudocode implementation of the Gauss-Jordan method takes a matrix A and a vector b as input and returns the solution vector x.
